rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port assumptions/guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE content, with type scopes for constants and equations, implementation scopes for assignments.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE expressions use only visible identifiers and maintain type consistency.
Use valid AGREE scalar types: int, real, bool for declarations.
Avoid referencing AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
Do not reference implementation-only subcomponents in component type contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses assume/guarantee/eq/const/assign with proper semicolons and labeled expressions.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference package-qualified identifiers not present in the model or its dependencies.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
AGREE annex placement in requirement analysis
